title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Secretary William Galvin agrees to produce reports on voting in jail after a lawsuit by Lawyers for Civil Rights.
The VOTES Act requires reports detailing voting access for nearly 9,000 eligible incarcerated voters in Massachusetts.
Galvin's office cited technical issues for delays in reports after 2024 and 2025 elections, now resolved by court settlement.

title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Reggie Gilliam, a former Buffalo Bills fullback, has signed a three-year contract with the New England Patriots.
Gilliam compares new Patriots quarterback Drake Maye to former MVP Josh Allen, citing similar physical attributes.
Gilliam aims to enhance the Patriots' running game with his blocking skills, aiding backs Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson.

title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romy Gonzalez to miss first two months of 2026 after shoulder surgery, was key player in 2025.
Gonzalez placed on 60-day IL; procedure deemed a 'clean-up,' averting major season-long concerns.
Red Sox sign lefty Danny Coulombe to a one-year deal to strengthen bullpen before Opening Day.

title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, a senior at Bishop Fenwick High School, is charged with murdering Janet Swallow at her Danvers home.
Prosecutors describe the murder as a 'random' attack with no known connection between DeMayo and Swallow.
DeMayo was found in Lynn with a knife, leading investigators to the crime scene on Amherst Street in Danvers.

title: Boston police seeking suspect who allegedly assaulted pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are seeking a suspect accused of assaulting a pregnant woman at a Roxbury laundromat on March 1.
The suspect allegedly kicked a five-month pregnant woman in an argument over a payment card at 104 Warren St.
Detectives seek a 6’0” light-skinned male suspect in a Yankees hat and a female associate with him.

title: Fired Mass. cop arrested in N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mikel Beltran, a former Lawrence police officer, was arrested in Londonderry, N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer.
Beltran, 37, was pulled over for flashing blue lights from his SUV and claimed to be a Lawrence officer.
Beltran was fired in March 2025 for discharging a handgun on duty and alleged misconduct, including making sexual remarks.

title: NFL insider believes Eagles will lower asking price to Patriots in A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are in talks to acquire A.J. Brown from the Eagles, who seek high draft picks in return.
Boston Sports Journal's Mike Giardi suggests the Eagles might lower their trade demands for A.J. Brown to New England.
Philadelphia could reduce its dead cap hit significantly by trading Brown after June 1, impacting trade timing.

title: Truck catches fire on Mass. Pike during early-morning commute, causing lane shutdowns
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A truck caught fire on the Massachusetts Turnpike in Westborough, closing westbound lanes Thursday morning.
Emergency crews extinguished the fire by 8:30 a.m. with assistance from Grafton firefighters.
Massachusetts Turnpike lanes reopened by 1:30 p.m.; no injuries were reported and the fire's cause is under investigation.

title: New Patriots WR Romeo Doubs explains why he chose New England in free agency
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romeo Doubs signed a four-year deal with the New England Patriots worth up to $80 million.
Doubs chose the Patriots to play with quarterback Drake Maye, praising his personality and potential as a top target.
Patriots VP Eliot Wolf highlighted Doubs' versatility and commitment, noting his active preparation using the playbook.

title: SUV owner experiences a serious – and not uncommon – engine issue
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2018 Hyundai Tucson owner experienced engine failure at 60,000 miles, requiring a full engine replacement under warranty.
Engine issues in certain Hyundai, Kia, Toyota, and Chevrolet models are attributed to manufacturing defects causing bearing failure.
Hyundai, Kia, Toyota, and General Motors are addressing engine failure risks through recalls and warranty replacements.
